title: Acyclicity

tex_statement.html: A CDMG $G=(J,V,E,L)$ is called acyclic if there does not exist any non-trivial directed walk from $v$ to itself in $G$ for any node $v \in G$.

lean_code_with_comments:
def IsAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) : Prop :=
  ∀ v ∈ G, ¬ ∃ p : Walk G v v, p.IsDirectedWalk ∧ 0 < p.length
lean_code_without_comments:
def IsAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) : Prop :=
  ∀ v ∈ G, ¬ ∃ p : Walk G v v, p.IsDirectedWalk ∧ 0 < p.length
addition_to_the_LN: [nontrivial_directed_walk_not_defined_in_block] A non-trivial directed walk is a directed walk $v_0 \tuh \cdots \tuh v_n$ with $n \ge 1$ (i.e., one that traverses at least one edge); the trivial walk consisting of a single node $v_0$ is excluded. In particular, a self-loop $v \tuh v$ constitutes a non-trivial directed walk from $v$ to itself, so an acyclic CDMG contains no self-loops on any $v \in V$.
design_choices:
## Design choices

**Live under the `CDMG` namespace as a field-style predicate.** Acyclicity inspects only the graph itself, so it belongs as `G.IsAcyclic` (dot-notation) alongside other graph-level fields like `CDMG.tuh / hut / huh / suh / hus / sus` and the structure projections `CDMG.hE_subset`. Vertex-parametric families (`Anc / Desc / Pa / Ch` from `def_3_5`) live one level up under `Causality` because they aren''t field-style predicates on `G`.

**Quantify with bounded `∀ v ∈ G, …` rather than over the ambient `Node` type or over `G.V`.** The bounded form desugars to `∀ v, v ∈ G → P v` with `∈` resolving via the `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance, which unfolds to `v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V` — the literal reading of "$v \in G$" in the LN. Quantifying over all of `Node` would obscure the LN faithfulness; restricting to `G.V` would silently drop the J-side. The J-half is automatically vacuous (the `hE_subset` field forces directed edge targets into `V`), so no expressive power is lost by following the LN literally.

**Phrase the body as `¬ ∃ p, P p` rather than `∀ p, ¬ P p`.** Logically equivalent, but `¬ ∃` mirrors the LN''s surface phrasing ("there does not exist any non-trivial directed walk"). Mathlib''s `SimpleGraph.IsAcyclic` uses the dual `∀ … ¬` shape, but the comparison is structural-only since `SimpleGraph` lacks the J/V split. Downstream consumers wanting the universal form get there in a single `rw [not_exists]`.

**Encode "non-trivial" as `0 < p.length`.** Without this guard, every CDMG would fail to be acyclic: the trivial walk `Walk.nil hv` satisfies `IsDirectedWalk` vacuously. The operator addition pins "non-trivial" to "at least one edge"; since `Walk.length` counts `cons` constructors, `0 < p.length` is the literal encoding. The author rejected `p ≠ .nil _` (requires data-equality reasoning) and `p.support.length ≠ 1` (vertex- rather than edge-count) in favor of the most compositional form for `Walk`-induction proofs that peel off a `cons`.

**Compose existing `Walk` + `IsDirectedWalk` primitives instead of introducing a bespoke `DirectedCycle` inductive.** Re-using the chapter-3 walk machinery means every walk lemma (`support`, `length`, `IsPath`, concat, reverse, sub-walk) is immediately available for acyclicity arguments; `IsDirectedWalk` does triple duty across ancestor reachability and bifurcation arms; and each preservation lemma for `do`, `split`, `swig`, marginalisation, and acyclification reduces to a single walk-correspondence statement rather than threading a parallel cycle-side infrastructure.

**Use a plain `def : Prop`, not `abbrev`, `class`, or a packaged `Decidable` instance.** An `abbrev` would inline at every use site and force `unfold IsAcyclic` downstream. A `class` would route through typeclass inference, which is wrong because acyclicity is a contingent property of a specific `G`, not a structural property of the type. Decidability is left out: it follows in principle from bounded search up to `(G.J ∪ G.V).card`, but no chapter-3 proof needs it, and `Classical.dec` is cheaper until a downstream `if G.IsAcyclic then …` triggers a real instance.

**Skip Mathlib coercions.** `SimpleGraph.IsAcyclic` and `DiGraph.IsAcyclic` exist but neither carries a J/V split or a bidirected channel; coercing would strip the J/V/L structure the rest of the chapter depends on, so the predicate is built directly from the in-repo `Walk` infrastructure.
